codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original runtime-stats feature (`1b3542d7`), the prior scrutiny finding, and the fix commit (`91fe6562`). The fix resolves the remaining non-blocking truthfulness issue: `/v0/runtime/stats.account_count` now counts only runtime-usable Codex accounts, mixed-provider and blank-key entries no longer inflate that value, and the TUI wording matches the runtime semantics.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skipped the rust-operator-worker skill's required manual `tuistory` verification even though the feature touched TUI-visible runtime wording. For this fix the unit-level wording assertion was likely sufficient, but the skill currently leaves that lightweight label-only case undocumented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill procedure step 6 requires manual verification with `tuistory` when the feature affects the TUI or runtime observability surface (`/home/fsos/code-lean/ai-tools/ProxyPilot/.factory/skills/rust-operator-worker/SKILL.md`). The handoff for session `38bb865f-5bfd-4b20-8a75-281170e69c06` records `followedProcedure: false` and says manual TUI verification was skipped in favor of unit tests because the change was only label alignment.

summary: Pass. The follow-up fix closes the prior `account_count` truthfulness gap and I found no new code issues in the scoped change.
